provenance.title: FutureSim: Replaying World Events to Evaluate Adaptive Agents
affiliations: Max Planck Institute for Intelligent Systems
Institute for AI, University of Stuttgart
provenance.abstract: AI agents are being increasingly deployed in dynamic, open-ended environments that require adapting to new information as it arrives. To efficiently measure this capability for realistic use-cases, we propose building grounded simulations that replay real-world events in the order they occurred. We build FutureSim, where agents forecast world events beyond their knowledge cutoff while interacting with a chronological replay of the world.
key_claims: FutureSim replays real-world events chronologically to evaluate long-horizon test-time adaptation in agents
Best frontier agent (GPT 5.5) achieves only 25% accuracy on world-event forecasting over a 3-month window
Open-weight frontier models show negative Brier Skill Score — worse than abstaining from prediction
Memory, agentic search, and inference scaling all provide measurable improvements in the simulation
The environment supports multi-agent dynamics and is reproducible, unlike live prediction-market evaluations

architecture.description: FutureSim is a simulation environment (not a neural architecture). It consists of: (1) a chronological event replay engine, (2) a forecasting task state manager (CSV-based), (3) a news corpus retrieval system (LanceDB + Qwen3-8B embeddings), and (4) a sandboxed agent execution environment.

components.name: Environment Engine
components.role: Manages simulation time progression and state updates

components.name: Task State Manager
components.role: Maintains CSV of forecasting questions with predictions and resolutions

components.name: News Corpus (CCNews)
components.role: Date-gated offline snapshot of 7.36M news articles from 141 sources (Jan 2023–Mar 2026)

components.name: Hybrid Retrieval System
components.role: LanceDB-backed semantic + keyword search over CCNews

components.name: Sandbox / Execution Environment
components.role: bwrap-isolated agent execution preventing future information leakage

components.name: Answer Matcher (DeepSeek v3.2)
components.role: LLM-based semantic answer equivalence checker for scoring

components.name: Question Generation Pipeline
components.role: Automated pipeline generating free-form forecasting questions from news articles
components.outputs: 330 forecasting questions with resolution dates and ground truths

agent_actions: submit_forecast(question_id, outcomes)  — register/update probability distribution over ≤5 outcomes
next_day()  — advance simulation by one day; updates task+context state

equations.name: Brier Skill Score
equations.role: Primary evaluation metric; proper scoring rule for subdistributions over free-form outcomes
equations.range: [-1, 1]; 1=perfect confident correct, 0=abstain, -1=all mass on wrong answer

symbols.Omega_q: set of outcomes submitted by agent for question q
symbols.p_q(o): probability assigned to outcome o (0 if not in Omega_q)
symbols.1[o=y_q]: indicator that outcome o matches ground truth

equations.name: Top-1 Accuracy
equations.role: Secondary metric; fraction of questions where top predicted outcome is correct

equations.name: Time-Weighted Score
equations.role: Rewards early accurate predictions; penalizes early confident wrong predictions

symbols.T_q: set of days question q is open
symbols.BSS_t(q): Brier Skill Score of prediction held on day t (0 if no forecast submitted)

equations.name: Time-Weighted Peer Score (multi-agent)
equations.role: Relative scoring in multi-agent setting; compares each agent to others on same question

equations.name: Aggregate Prediction (multi-agent)
equations.role: Coordinate-wise mean of agent forecasts, exposed as crowd aggregate each day

equations.name: Total Variation Distance (multi-agent divergence)
equations.role: Measures divergence between agent predictions over time

mathematical_spec.properness_proof: BSS is proven strictly proper for subdistributions (Theorem C.1 in paper); expected score uniquely maximized when p = pi_bar (projection of true belief onto reported outcome set)

tensor_semantics.note: FutureSim is a simulation environment, not a deep learning model. Tensor semantics apply to the retrieval embedding component.

training_pipeline.note: No training pipeline — FutureSim is a benchmarking/simulation framework, not a trained model. Agents are evaluated at inference time only.

question_generation.source: Al Jazeera articles from CCNews, Q1 2026
steps: LLM generates free-form short-answer question from timestamped article
Leakage filter: remove if answerable before Jan 1 2026 (GPT 5.5 + web search judges)
Difficulty filter: remove if too stale/easy or if still unanswerable as of April 2026
Resolution date repair: set to earliest date answer could be confidently inferred

key_ablations: Test-time adaptation: fix initial predictions to worst agent, compare improvement
Memory ablation: remove write access to memory files
Search ablation: daily context updates vs. frozen corpus vs. single query vs. agentic search
Inference scaling: GPT 5.5 at none/low/medium/high/xhigh reasoning effort
Multi-agent: 3 simultaneous DeepSeek V3.2 agents with shared aggregate prediction

reported_results.memory_benefit: All 3 tested models perform worse without memory (qualitative traces show storage of post-resolution feedback)
reported_results.inference_scaling: Consistent improvement from none → high reasoning effort; diminishing returns at xhigh

implementation_assumptions.assumption: LanceDB version not specified; assumed latest stable compatible with Qwen3-8B embedding dimension
implementation_assumptions.basis: Common practice for vector DB integration

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Qwen3 Embedding 8B produces fixed-size embeddings (likely 4096-dim based on model size)
implementation_assumptions.basis: Inferred from model architecture; paper does not state embedding dimension

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Hybrid retrieval weights for semantic vs keyword search not specified; assume equal weighting or LanceDB default
implementation_assumptions.basis: Paper says 'hybrid semantic + keyword search' without specifying fusion method

implementation_assumptions.assumption: bwrap sandbox configuration details beyond described rules inferred from standard practice
implementation_assumptions.basis: Paper describes high-level sandboxing rules; implementation details in appendix are partial

implementation_assumptions.assumption: CCNews articles stored as JSONL files organized by date (articles/YYYY/MM/DD/articles.jsonl)
implementation_assumptions.basis: Explicitly stated in prompt templates (Appendix E.1)

implementation_assumptions.assumption: DeepSeek V3.2 answer matching prompt temperature set to 0 (deterministic) for reproducibility
implementation_assumptions.basis: Standard practice for evaluation judges; not specified in paper

ambiguities.description: Exact LanceDB version, embedding dimension, and hybrid retrieval fusion method not specified
ambiguities.primary_assumption: Latest stable LanceDB, Qwen3-8B default embedding dim, equal weight fusion

ambiguities.description: Full bwrap configuration flags not provided; only high-level sandboxing behavior described
ambiguities.primary_assumption: Standard namespace isolation with bind mounts for article directories

ambiguities.description: Outcome set size limit of ≤5 per question enforced at answer-matching time, unclear if enforced at submission time
ambiguities.primary_assumption: Enforced at submission time via environment validation
